description: Wave E2 (debt #11 follow-up) long-form scenario stress-testing the commitment lifecycle and plan-intent owners. Ramp (1-5), task framing (6-12), CREATE+REVISE on a multi-step plan (13-19), an explicit DEFER (20-24), pickup + COMPLETE (25-30), reflection (31-34). 34 turns, regime trajectory: problem_solving / guided_exploration with brief casual_social anchors so commitment alignment proposals have something to argue about.

turns.user_input: Hey, I have a side project I keep starting and stopping. Can we work through it together?
It is a small CLI tool to dedupe my photo library, but I keep getting stuck on the matching part.
I have about three weekends per month I can give it, plus a couple of evenings.
Goal would be to actually finish a working v0.1 by the end of the quarter. I want a real release, not a half-thing.
I have tried to scope it before but kept piling features on. This time I want a hard cut.
Okay, here is the must-have list: hash-based exact match, perceptual hash for near-dupes, dry-run mode, and a manual review screen.
Can we agree those four are v0.1 and everything else (cloud sync, face dedup, video) is v0.2?
Great. Then I commit: I will build those four features over the next eight weeks.
Eight weeks is real because I checked the calendar. There are no big trips and only one work conference.
Walk me through how to break those four into weekly chunks. I want to see if it actually fits.
Week 1 hash matching, week 2 perceptual hash, week 3 dry run, week 4 review screen, weeks 5-8 polish. Wait, where does testing live?
Yeah, we should bake testing into each week, not at the end. Otherwise nothing actually ships.
Okay, revised: each feature gets two weeks instead of one, with the second week being tests + small polish.
But that is eight weeks just for the four features, with no buffer for stuck moments.
Hmm. Three weeks per feature would be safer, four features = twelve weeks. That is more than the quarter.
Trade-off time. Either I push the deadline to Q2-mid, or I drop the manual review screen to v0.2.
Let me revise the commitment: keep four features, push the delivery to twelve weeks, accept the slip.
Honesty over fake aggressive deadlines. Twelve weeks it is.
Okay, week one starts Saturday. I will share progress here at the end of each week.
Hi, end of week one. I have to defer the project for ten days. Family thing came up unexpectedly.
It is fine, just an aging-parent logistics thing. Not a crisis. Just needs my full attention this stretch.
I want to be explicit: the v0.1 commitment is paused, not abandoned. Resuming on the 14th.
Please do not let me forget. Holding myself to the resume date matters more than the original deadline at this point.
Honestly the deferring is what kills these projects normally. I want a clean DEFER not a silent fade.
Back. Resumed yesterday. Hash matcher works on a 3000-photo test set, 0 false positives so far.
Perceptual hash is harder than expected. Two near-dupes shot seconds apart are coming up as different.
I think I am over-tuning. Let me ship the simpler version and accept some misses for v0.1.
Dry run mode landed today. It just prints what would be deleted, no actual delete. Confidence-building feature.
Manual review screen ended up being a tiny TUI. Less polish, but ships.
Okay, all four features merged. Tests passing. Tagging v0.1 right now. Closing the commitment.
I want to mark this as COMPLETED, not just abandoned. Real release, GitHub tag, README updated.
Reflecting a bit: the DEFER mid-project was the make-or-break moment. Naming it kept it from becoming a fade-out.
If we do another round of this, I want to bake DEFER + RESUME directly into the plan, not as an exception.
Thanks for keeping me honest. Same project shape but a v0.2 conversation can wait until I have actually used v0.1 for a month.
